message:
fix(unix): return io::Error instead of panicking on poisoned registration lock in UnixListener

The UnixListener::register_interest() method used .expect() on the
Mutex lock result, which would panic and crash the runtime if the lock
was ever poisoned (e.g., by a panic in another thread while holding the
lock). Replaced with .map_err() returning io::ErrorKind::Other so the
error propagates gracefully through the poll chain.

Added test verifying that after deliberately poisoning the registration
lock, poll_next returns Poll::Ready(Some(Err)) with ErrorKind::Other
rather than panicking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
